content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                    Date : May 11, 1989


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       John G. McLean

Subject : Advice No. T-89-282


       Kathy and I discussed this mailer submitted by Bob Leidigh.
We concluded that it fit within the exception for standard
letterhead forms or envelopes. Because of the need for a quick
response, Bob said he only needed telephone advice.

Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG

May 9, 1989


HAND DELIVERED

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: SUBMISSION OF EXHIBIT FOR TELEPHONE ADVICE REQUEST

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     Pursuant to our telephone discussion of last Friday,
I am submitting for your review a mock-up of a self-
mailer.  You stated that you would be unable to provide
me with telephone advice without being able to look at
the document.

     The name of the elected officer will appear in only
the two places shown and will not appear (nor will any
photograph or other reference) anywhere else in the
document.  We do not seek advice regarding the text of
the document (which is signified by the blank lines).

     My request on behalf of my client the Senate Rules
Committee relates solely to the issue of whether such a
self-mailer is permissible as opposed to a oft-denied,
two-sheet memorandum stuffed into an envelope which bears
the same letterhead and the same return address.  (I
pointed out that postcards had been approved in the
Stirling Advice Letter, No. A-89-132.)

     Both you and John McLean agreed that the latter was
permissible under Regulation 18901, assuming that
"standard" stationery and envelopes are used.  However,
you were reluctant to conclude that the self-mailer would
be permissible without seeing it.  Therefore, I am
providing it to you.

     My client is very anxious to receive a telephonic
response to this request.  Pending your response, use of
the self-mailer format is being held up and the separate

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY V. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDICH

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD G. CONNELLY Member
California State Legislature

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280

Kathryn Donovan
May 9, 1989
Page two


sheet method is being used.  This increases the cost of the
mailings by approximately 45% over the cost of printing and
addressing the self-mailer format.

I await your early response.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG

[signature] ROBERT E. LEIDIGH

cc: Senate Rules Committee

r

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 11, 1989


Robert E. Leidigh
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Letter No. 89-282

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on May 9, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assurance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

SENATOR MARY SMITH
2234 La Mesa Way., Suite 200
San Jose, CA 98009

Reduced Size
Actual Size
Ledger

JOE BILLARD
3324 Pecord Drive
Sacramento, CA 95487

sections.conclusion: The mailer was concluded to fit within the exception for standard letterhead forms or envelopes under Regulation 18901, making the self-mailer format permissible.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a self-mailer format containing an elected officer's name in two locations complies with Political Reform Act regulations as an alternative to a four-sided, two-sheet memorandum in an envelope with standard letterhead.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the self-mailer format fits within the exception for standard letterhead forms or envelopes under Regulation 18901 and is therefore permissible.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document combines multiple parts: incoming request letter, FPPC acknowledgment letter, and internal memorandum. No formal Q&C sections present. Telephone advice was provided but the conclusion is documented in the internal memorandum rather than a formal written response. Year and document ID clearly stated as 89-282 and 1989.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a self-mailer format containing an elected officer's name in two locations complies with Political Reform Act regulations as an alternative to a four-sided, two-sheet memorandum in an envelope with standard letterhead.

CONCLUSION: The mailer was concluded to fit within the exception for standard letterhead forms or envelopes under Regulation 18901, making the self-mailer format permissible.

embedding.summary: This document comprises an incoming request from attorney Robert E. Leidigh submitted May 9, 1989 regarding whether a self-mailer format is permissible under Political Reform Act regulations, followed by an FPPC acknowledgment and a memorandum documenting that FPPC staff concluded the mailer complies with the standard letterhead exception.
